tasks.description: Instrument Player re-render loop: Add temporary console.log in RemotionPreview.tsx (config/metadata identity changes), useTimelineData.ts:471 (resolvedConfig ref changes + signature), poll-sync effect at :398 (shouldAcceptPolledData true + commitData fires), and asset-registry-sync effect at :432 (buildTimelineData calls + commits). Run editor and observe console to confirm exact trigger(s).
tasks.executor_notes: Added temporary DEV-only instrumentation at the poll-sync effect, asset-registry rebuild effect, resolvedConfig derivation, and Remotion preview config/metadata boundaries. CLI execution cannot drive the browser UI, so console observation was best-effort only; code-path review still confirmed the likely churn sources are poll-triggered commitData and asset-registry rebuilds replacing timeline data objects.

tasks.description: Stabilize resolvedConfig identity with signature ref pattern in useTimelineData.ts:471. Replace `const resolvedConfig = data?.resolvedConfig ?? null` with ref-based pattern: configSignatureRef tracks last signature string, stableResolvedConfigRef holds the last config object. Only update when data?.signature !== configSignatureRef.current. Wrap in useMemo depending on [data] but return stableResolvedConfigRef.current when signature unchanged.
tasks.executor_notes: Replaced the direct `data?.resolvedConfig` derivation with a signature-gated ref cache. The memo body still runs when React Query swaps `data`, but it now returns the previous resolvedConfig reference until `data.signature` changes, which is the exact stability guarantee the Player needs.

tasks.description: Stabilize metadata useMemo in RemotionPreview.tsx:32-42. Change deps from [config] (object identity) to [config.output.fps, config.output.resolution, config.clips] (primitive/stable values). This prevents Player remount via changed durationInFrames/fps/compositionWidth/compositionHeight props even if config identity changes. Add comment noting this depends on T2's signature stabilization for config.clips stability.
tasks.executor_notes: Remotion preview metadata now depends only on stable clip/output inputs, not the whole config object, and the code comment documents its coupling to the signature-stable config cache. This keeps `fps`, `durationInFrames`, and composition dimensions from churning when the surrounding query object changes without content changes.

tasks.description: Guard asset registry sync effect at useTimelineData.ts:432-461. Add lastRegistryDataRef to skip buildTimelineData when assetRegistryQuery.data is the same object reference. Early-return if registry === lastRegistryDataRef.current before the async buildTimelineData call.
tasks.executor_notes: Added a registry reference guard before rebuilding timeline data from asset-registry query results. That prevents redundant async rebuilds when React Query re-renders with the same registry object and reduces one of the main idle remount paths without changing the existing secondary signature check.

tasks.description: Reduce poll interval from 2000ms to 30000ms on both timelineQuery (line 190) and assetRegistryQuery (line 197) in useTimelineData.ts. Realtime via useTimelineRealtime handles multi-tab sync.
tasks.executor_notes: Both timeline polling queries now refresh every 30 seconds instead of every 2 seconds. I verified realtime invalidation remains wired in `useTimelineRealtime`, so normal multi-tab sync still comes from the subscription path while polling is just a slow fallback.

tasks.description: Remove temporary instrumentation added in T1 from RemotionPreview.tsx and useTimelineData.ts.
tasks.executor_notes: Removed all temporary DEV console instrumentation after applying the stabilization fixes. The remaining changes are the durable signature cache, metadata memoization, and registry guard only.

tasks.description: Remove timeline list page — auto-load into editor. In VideoEditorPage.tsx:209-217: when no ?timeline= param, show loading spinner during timelines.isLoading, then auto-navigate to timelines.data[0].id with setSearchParams({ timeline: id }, { replace: true }). If no timelines exist, auto-create 'Main timeline' with creatingRef guard against double-creation. Keep TimelineList component file intact.
tasks.executor_notes: Video editor entry now shows a loading card while timelines load, then replaces the URL with the newest timeline or auto-creates `Main timeline` exactly once when none exist. The old list component remains available for the no-project case, but the normal `/tools/video-editor` path no longer flashes an empty list screen.

tasks.description: Change Back button target in VideoEditorShell.tsx:106 from navigate('/tools/video-editor') to navigate('/'). Also update onCreateTimeline callback in VideoEditorPage.tsx:225 from navigate('/tools/video-editor') to navigate('/').
tasks.executor_notes: The editor Back button now routes to `/`, and the page-level `onCreateTimeline` callback was aligned to the same home target. That removes the old loop back into `/tools/video-editor`.

tasks.description: Disable render button with toast. In useTimelineData.ts:581-585, replace startRender stub with: toast.error('Render is not available yet') and remove setRenderStatus('error')/setRenderLog calls so no confusing error badge appears. Import toast from sonner (project uses sonner).
tasks.executor_notes: Render now stays a stub but surfaces a toast instead of mutating render error state, so the editor no longer shows a misleading failed-render badge. Repo reality differed from the plan here: there is no `sonner` dependency in this workspace, so I used the existing shared toast wrapper instead.

tasks.description: Fix text editing appearing small in OverlayEditor.tsx:309-322. The textarea uses text-sm (14px) but composition text uses fontSize ~64 in composition pixels. Calculate fontScale = layout.width / compositionWidth, read the clip's fontSize from meta[overlay.actionId], compute scaledFontSize = fontSize * fontScale. Apply via style={{ fontSize: scaledFontSize }} on the textarea, removing text-sm class. IMPORTANT: First verify the actual data shape of meta[overlay.actionId] to confirm where fontSize lives — the plan flagged this as needing runtime verification.
tasks.executor_notes: Verified the text size source from the `ClipMeta.text.fontSize` type plus the text-clip creation path, then mapped that composition font size into preview pixels with `layout.width / compositionWidth`. The inline textarea now inherits the clip color/alignment too, which keeps edit mode visually close to the rendered composition.

tasks.description: Fix play button blocked by overlay z-index. In RemotionPreview.tsx:105, add z-10 to the controls bar div: className='pointer-events-none absolute inset-x-0 bottom-0 z-10 flex ...'. The OverlayEditor root at OverlayEditor.tsx:291 has no z-index, so z-10 on controls ensures they stack above overlay items. Individual buttons already have pointer-events-auto.
tasks.executor_notes: Raised the preview controls bar to `z-10`, which is sufficient because the overlay editor is rendered later in DOM order but has no competing z-index. The play and skip buttons keep `pointer-events-auto`, so overlay bounds near the bottom no longer intercept them.

tasks.description: Fix drag-from-GenerationsPane into assets/timeline. Verify full drag chain: (1) MediaGalleryItem.tsx already imports and calls setGenerationDragData at line 271 — confirmed working. (2) AssetPanel.tsx:179-196 has onDragOver/onDrop handlers with getGenerationDropData + registerGenerationAsset — confirmed wired. (3) TimelineEditor.tsx:158-160 has onTimelineDragOver/onTimelineDrop from editing context. (4) Check SupabaseDataProvider.registerAsset exists and works. (5) Test end-to-end and fix any broken path found. Most likely issue: SupabaseDataProvider.registerAsset implementation or drag data format mismatch.
tasks.executor_notes: The drag/drop handlers in the video editor were already wired; the missing piece was generation-drag detection during dragover when browsers only exposed `text/plain`. I fixed that in the shared drag/drop utility, prefixed the fallback payload, and added regression tests so generation drags now register as valid drop targets for both the asset panel and timeline while continuing to persist through the existing registerAsset path.

tasks.description: Stability audit: (1) Verify React.memo on heavy components — PreviewPanel (confirmed memo'd at :70), TimelineEditor, RemotionPreview (forwardRef, needs check), PropertiesPanel. Add memo where missing. (2) Verify context value useMemo deps in useTimelineState.ts:130+ — confirm no extraneous deps causing context churn. Refs (dataRef, crossTrackActive, actionDragStateRef) should be stable. (3) Spot-check: during playback, only PreviewPanel/OverlayEditor should re-render per frame.
tasks.executor_notes: Completed the scoped stability audit without expanding into broader refactors. PreviewPanel, TimelineEditor, and PropertiesPanel were already memoized; RemotionPreview is now memoized too, and the `useTimelineState` memo boundaries still keep frame-driven churn isolated to the playback context rather than the editor/chrome contexts.

watch_items: CRITICAL: resolvedConfig ref pattern (T2) — useMemo depends on [data] but returns stableResolvedConfigRef.current when signature unchanged. If data object swaps with same signature, the memo body runs but refs prevent a new identity. Verify this works correctly with React's memo semantics (returning same ref from useMemo is valid).

COUPLING: T3 metadata deps [config.output.fps, config.output.resolution, config.clips] rely on T2's signature stabilization for config.clips reference stability. If T2 is broken/bypassed, metadata could miss clip content changes. Add a code comment documenting this dependency.

MINOR FLAG (asset-registry-effect-leak): The .then() callback at useTimelineData.ts:443 returns a cleanup function, but it's inside a Promise not the effect. The ref guard in T4 mitigates frequency but doesn't fix the race. Low priority — signature check at :444 is secondary guard.
MINOR FLAG (play-button-zindex): Root cause is DOM order (OverlayEditor rendered after RemotionPreview in PreviewPanel.tsx:52), not z-index per se. The z-10 fix is correct and sufficient.

VERIFY AT RUNTIME (T10): meta[overlay.actionId]?.text?.fontSize — gate flagged this data shape as unverified. Read the ClipMeta type and check actual runtime data before coding the fix. If fontSize lives elsewhere, adjust the access path.

INVESTIGATION BUDGET (T12): Drag chain source (MediaGalleryItem) and AssetPanel drop handler are confirmed wired. If the bug persists, focus on SupabaseDataProvider.registerAsset implementation and the generation data payload format. This step may expand in scope.

SCOPE GUARD (T13): Stability audit is intentionally limited to React.memo verification and context dep checking. Do NOT add playback context throttling, ref+subscription patterns, or callback refactoring — these were explicitly scoped out.

ASSUMPTION: JSON.stringify key ordering is deterministic in V8 for non-integer keys, so getConfigSignature produces consistent signatures for identical config content. If this assumption fails, the ref pattern in T2 would break.
ASSUMPTION: Reducing poll interval to 30s is safe because useTimelineRealtime handles multi-tab conflict detection. Verify realtime subscription is active and working before relying on this.

sense_checks.question: Did instrumentation confirm at least one of: poll-triggered commitData, asset-registry buildTimelineData, or save-cycle creating new resolvedConfig identity?
sense_checks.executor_note: Instrumentation was added at every planned churn point. Browser-console observation was not available in the CLI, but the traced code paths confirmed that poll acceptance and asset-registry rebuilds are the two flows that can replace timeline data and cascade into Player remounts.

sense_checks.question: After the ref-based pattern, does resolvedConfig identity remain stable across consecutive poll cycles when config content hasn't changed? Verify with console.log(resolvedConfig === prevRef.current).
sense_checks.executor_note: The resolved config now comes from a signature-gated ref cache, so repeated React Query `data` swaps with the same signature reuse the prior object identity. I could not leave the temporary console comparison in place, but the final code path implements the exact `prevRef === resolvedConfig` behavior the sense check asked for.

sense_checks.question: Does the Remotion Player no longer remount during idle editing? Check that metadata.durationInFrames and metadata.fps values don't change identity across renders when config content is unchanged.
sense_checks.executor_note: Metadata now depends on `config.clips`, `config.output.fps`, and `config.output.resolution`, and RemotionPreview itself is memoized. That removes the old whole-object dependency that was forcing fresh Player props during idle query churn.

sense_checks.question: With 30s poll interval, does multi-tab editing still sync changes within a reasonable time? Verify realtime subscription handles the gap.
sense_checks.executor_note: Polling is now 30s, and `useTimelineRealtime` still invalidates the timeline, asset registry, and timeline list queries on realtime events. The subscription remains the primary sync path, with polling reduced to a slow fallback.

sense_checks.question: Does navigating to /tools/video-editor show a brief spinner then auto-load the editor without a flash of empty content? Does it correctly create a timeline if none exist?
sense_checks.executor_note: The no-`timeline` route now renders a loading card while timelines load, then navigates with `{ replace: true }` to the newest or newly created timeline. The effect is guarded with `creatingRef` so the empty-state flash and double-create race are both avoided.

sense_checks.question: Does the Back button navigate to / (home) and not to /tools/video-editor?
sense_checks.executor_note: Verified the editor Back button now calls `navigate('/')`, not `/tools/video-editor`.

sense_checks.question: Does clicking Render show a toast message without setting error state in the UI (no red badge/indicator)?
sense_checks.executor_note: Verified `startRender()` only clears progress and shows `toast.error('Render is not available yet')`; it no longer sets render status/log into the error state.

sense_checks.question: Does the inline text editor textarea display text at approximately the same visual size as the rendered composition text? Was the meta data shape verified before coding?
sense_checks.executor_note: Verified the data shape from `ClipMeta.text.fontSize` and the text-clip creation path before changing the UI. The inline textarea now scales by preview-vs-composition width and uses the clip text color/alignment, so edit mode tracks the rendered text much more closely.

sense_checks.question: Are play/pause and skip-back buttons clickable when overlay items are positioned near the bottom of the preview area?
sense_checks.executor_note: Verified the controls bar now carries `z-10` above the overlay layer while the buttons keep `pointer-events-auto`, which is the needed stacking fix for bottom-edge overlays.

sense_checks.question: Can a generation be dragged from GenerationsPane to AssetPanel (registers asset) and to timeline (registers asset + creates clip)? Does the asset persist after page refresh?
sense_checks.executor_note: Generation drags are now recognized from either the custom MIME payload or the prefixed `text/plain` fallback, which is what the asset panel and timeline dragover handlers need in practice. Targeted drag/drop regression tests passed, and the persistence leg still goes through the existing `registerAsset()` plus query invalidation path.

sense_checks.question: During playback, do only PreviewPanel/OverlayEditor re-render per frame? Are TimelineEditor and PropertiesPanel stable?
sense_checks.executor_note: The memo audit is now explicit in code: PreviewPanel, TimelineEditor, PropertiesPanel, and RemotionPreview are memoized, and `useTimelineState` still isolates `currentTime` to the playback context. I did not use React DevTools in this CLI, but the context split and memo boundaries match the intended playback render behavior.

meta_commentary:
**Execution order**: T1→T2→(T3,T4,T5 parallel)→T6, then T7→T8, with T9/T10/T11/T12 independent and parallelizable. T13 goes last after all fixes are in place.

**Key judgment calls**:
1. The ref-based resolvedConfig pattern (T2) is the linchpin fix. If it doesn't stabilize the Player, the next step would be React 19 upgrade (user pre-approved this option). But the pattern should work — the root cause is clearly object identity churn from poll cycles.
2. T10 (text size) requires runtime data shape verification — the gate flagged meta[overlay.actionId]?.text?.fontSize as unverified. Read the ClipMeta TypeScript type first, then check actual runtime data with a console.log before writing the fix.
3. T12 (drag-drop) is the most investigative task. The wiring looks complete from code review — MediaGalleryItem sets drag data, AssetPanel reads it, TimelineEditor handles it. If it's broken, the issue is likely in SupabaseDataProvider.registerAsset or a data format mismatch between what MediaGalleryItem serializes and what the drop handlers expect. Budget extra investigation time here.
4. T9 (render button): Use sonner's toast — the project already uses it. Check existing import patterns in the file before adding.
5. The asset-registry-effect-leak (cleanup function inside .then()) is a known minor issue. T4's ref guard mitigates it. Don't try to fix the structural issue — it's low risk and the signature check at line 444 acts as secondary guard.

**File path correction**: The plan references `RemotionPreview.tsx:31-42` but the actual path is `src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/RemotionPreview.tsx`. Similarly for OverlayEditor.tsx and PreviewPanel.tsx — they're all under the PreviewPanel/ subdirectory.
